title: Bill to Protect Low Income Homeowners from Skyrocketing HOA Fees Heads to the Governor’s Desk
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem
raw_preview:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assemblymember Haney’s AB 572 caps annual HOA fees at 5% plus the rate of inflation or 10%, whichever is less, for below market rate homeowners.",
    "The bill prohibits steep homeowner association (HOA) fee increases that push low income homeowners into foreclosure.",
raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assemblymember Haney’s AB 572 caps annual HOA fees at 5% plus the rate of inflation or 10%, whichever is less, for below market rate homeowners.",
    "The bill prohibits steep homeowner association (HOA) fee increases that push low income homeowners into foreclosure.",
    "Current CA law sets the maximum HOA fee increase at 20% for all homeowners regardless of income level.",
    "After only a few years of large fee increases, below market rate homeowners’ HOA fees can be higher than their entire mortgage.",
    "Market rate homeowners can recoup the costs of HOA fees through the eventual sale of their unit at a new, appreciated price.",
    "Below Market Rate units do not have the same luxury as market rate homeowners in recouping HOA costs due to their listing price restrictions."
  ]
}
```
